DISCLAIMER: Under construction, use the results with caution.

parallel_workers.description: How many parallel workers should Task Manager use

export_input_data_to_csv.description: Select this flag to export the input data pool into a CSV file for all tasks.

input_data_csv_export_path.description: The path to save the exported input data CSV file.

input_data_csv_import_path.description: If user wish to compare the input data with previous runs, the user can pass in the previously saved input data CSV path. The result will be combined together for a side-by-side comparison.

tasks.description: The list of things that Task Manager should do in an unordered manner

task_type.description: What kind of task is this?

metadata_file_path.description: The path to the metadata JSON files to load and use in the Input Manager of this task

input_root.description: Root directory for all input files.

properties_file_path.description: The path to the first metadata properties JSON file to load and compare to the other metadata file loaded

comparison_properties_file_path.description: The path to the second metadata properties JSON file to load and compare to the other metadata file loaded

convert_variable_table_path.description: The path to the CSV file that contains the previous and the new name of variables.

output_prefix.description: Will be used to generate a name for each of the output files for this task.

log_verbosity.description: Specifies the level of log type to be printed on console.

variable_name_style.description: The formatting option for variable_names.txt file

init_herd.description: Select this flag if you want to initialize the herd by generating a herd population through simulation.

save_animals.description: Choose this flag if you want to save the generated herd data into a JSON file.

save_animals_directory.description: The saving directory for animals if save_animals is set to true

filters_directory.description: The directory for the files containing the output filters

csv_output_directory.description: The directory for the csv output files to be saved

json_output_directory.description: The directory for the JSON output files to be saved.

report_directory.description: The directory for reports to be saved to.

logs_directory.description: The directory for saving log message files, variable names, and other simulation outputs to.

suppress_log_files.description: If true, logs will not be written as output files.

run_eee.description: Run the emissions, energy, and economics estimators prior to post-processing.

load_saved_output_pools.description: Load previously saved output pools prior to post-processing.

saved_output_pools.description: Collection of saved output pools to reload before post-processing.

name.description: Identifier applied to variables loaded from this pool.

path.description: File path to the saved output pool JSON file.

output_pool_path.description: The path to a JSON files to load the Output Manager's pool from for post processing

random_seed.description: The random seed, set it to 0 to auto-generate it

chunkification.description: Whether to periodically save the output variable pool to a file.

save_chunk_threshold_call_count.description: The threshold add variable call count for saving the output variable pool chunk.

maximum_memory_usage.description: The maximum memory usage in Bytes.

maximum_memory_usage_percent.description: The maximum percentage of memory use. If save_chunk_threshold_call_count is provided, then that is what is used to determine when to chunk, otherwise it is maximum memory usage, otherwise it is max memory usage percent.

multi_run_counts.description: If task type is multi_run, indicates how many single simulations should run.

skip_values.description: The number of points in the sampling sequence to skip for Sensitivity Analysis

sampler_n.description: The number of samples to generate for Sensitivity Analysis. Ideally a power of 2 and >= 'skip_values'

SA_input_variables.description: The input variables to perform Sensitivity Analysis on

variable_name.description: The name of the variable to be sampled, has to match within Input Manager Pool

data_type.description: Specifies the data type of this variable

SA_load_balancing_start.description: The lower bound value for sensitivity analysis load balancing

SA_load_balancing_stop.description: The upper bound value for sensitivity analysis load balancing
